perturbation.original_text: 6 Notices All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be sent by first -class registered or recorded delivery post to the party being served at its address specified above or at such other address of which such party shall have given notice as aforesaid, and marked for the attention of that party's signatory of this Agreement. The date of service shall be deemed to be the day following the day on which the notice was transmitted or posted as the case m ay be.
perturbation.changed_text: 6 Notices All notices under this Agreement can be communicated verbally or via email. The date of service shall be deemed to be the day of conversation or the exact date the email was sent.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates 'written' notices sent by registered or recorded delivery. The modified version permits verbal or email communication. This contradicts the enforceability of notices, as verbal agreements are difficult to prove and emails can be easily disavowed. This in-text contradicts the original written format of the notices, now making it difficult to validate. The modification of the section creates a contradiction that undermines the formal procedures set out in the original notice clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6 Notices All notices under this Agreement can be communicated verbally or via email. The date of service shall be deemed to be the day of conversation or the exact date the email was sent.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates written notices via registered or recorded delivery, while the changed text allows verbal or email communication. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written notices, creating a strong contradiction and making it difficult to validate notices. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, re-emphasizing the contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4 Disclaimer and warranty (a) Each party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by either party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4 Disclaimer and warranty (a) Each party transfers all rights in its Confidential Information to the other party and all rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, a licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by either party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly reserves all rights in Confidential Information and disclaims any implied license. The modified version states that each party transfers all rights to the other, creating an explicit contradiction. The clause originally did not grant license, the added perturbation implies to grant license, which is contrary to the original. This in-text contradiction fundamentally alters the ownership and usage rights of the Confidential Information. This creates uncertainty and disputes over intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4 Disclaimer and warranty (a) Each party transfers all rights in its Confidential Information to the other party and all rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, a licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by either party.
perturbation.justification: The original text reserves all rights in confidential information and disclaims any implied license. The changed text states that each party transfers all rights to the other and grants a license, creating an explicit and direct conflict. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Therefore, the change causes a clear in-text contradiction, as the same section of the contract both grants and denies rights related to confidential information and licensing.
